content.content:
1→(* FVI (Find Vector Intersection) pure geometry functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d1/fvi.cpp. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effects for on-demand data fetching. OCaml FVI fetches segment/object
     5→   data only for segments actually visited, instead of pre-packing everything. *)
     6→type _ Effect.t +=
     7→  | Fetch_object_data : int -> int array Effect.t
     8→  | Fetch_collision_table : int array Effect.t
     9→
    10→type vec3 = int * int * int
    11→
    12→let ij_table = [| [| 2; 1 |]; [| 0; 2 |]; [| 1; 0 |] |]
    13→let it_none = 0
    14→let it_face = 1
    15→let it_edge = 2
    16→let it_point = 3
    17→
    18→(* Wrap native int to signed 32-bit range, matching C int32_t overflow. *)
    19→let wrap32 x = Ox_math.wrap_i64_to_fix (Int64.of_int x)
    20→
    21→(* Overflow guard: returns true if abs(a)*abs(b) would overflow bit 47+. *)
    22→let oflow_check ~a ~b =
    23→  let tmp = Int64.(abs (of_int a) * abs (of_int b)) in
    24→  Int64.(shift_right tmp 47 <> 0L)
    25→;;
    26→
    27→(* Find where line p0->p1 intersects plane (plane_pnt, plane_norm),
    28→   accounting for radius offset. Returns Some newp if found, None if parallel/no-hit. *)
    29→let find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad =
    30→  let open Ox_math in
    31→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
    32→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:p0 ~b:plane_pnt in
    33→  let num_raw = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:w in
    34→  let den = wrap32 (-vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:d) in
    35→  let num = wrap32 (num_raw - rad) in
    36→  (* Check for various bad values — C operator precedence:
    37→     (den == 0) || ((den > 0) && ((num > den) || ((-num >> 15) >= den)))
    38→                 || (den < 0 && num < den) *)
    39→  let neg_num = wrap32 (-num) in
    40→  if den = 0
    41→  then None
    42→  else if den > 0 && (num > den || neg_num asr 15 >= den)
    43→  then None
    44→  else if den < 0 && num < den
    45→  then None
    46→  else (
    47→    let f1_0 = 0x10000 in
    48→    if abs num / (f1_0 / 2) >= abs den
    49→    then None (* would overflow *)
    50→    else (
    51→      let k = fixdiv ~a:num ~b:den in
    52→      let dx, dy, dz = d in
    53→      if oflow_check ~a:dx ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dy ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dz ~b:k
    54→      then None
    55→      else (
    56→        let scaled = vm_vec_scale2 ~v:d ~n:num ~d:den in
    57→        let newp = vm_vec_add ~a:p0 ~b:scaled in
    58→        Some (k, newp))))
    59→;;
    60→
    61→(* 2D point-in-polygon test. Projects face onto a 2D plane using ij_table
    62→   based on the largest component of the normal. Returns an edgemask where
    63→   bit N is set if the point is outside edge N. *)
    64→let check_point_to_face ~checkp ~norm ~nv ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
    65→  let open Ox_math in
    66→  let cpx, cpy, cpz = checkp in
    67→  let cp = [| cpx; cpy; cpz |] in
    68→  let nx, ny, nz = norm in
    69→  let n = [| nx; ny; nz |] in
    70→  let tx = abs nx in
    71→  let ty = abs ny in
    72→  let tz = abs nz in
    73→  let biggest = if tx > ty then if tx > tz then 0 else 2 else if ty > tz then 1 else 2 in
    74→  let i, j =
    75→    if n.(biggest) > 0
    76→    then ij_table.(biggest).(0), ij_table.(biggest).(1)
    77→    else ij_table.(biggest).(1), ij_table.(biggest).(0)
    78→  in
    79→  let check_i = cp.(i) in
    80→  let check_j = cp.(j) in
    81→  let edgemask = ref 0 in
    82→  for edge = 0 to nv - 1 do
    83→    let vx0, vy0, vz0 = vertex_positions.(edge) in
    84→    let v0 = [| vx0; vy0; vz0 |] in
    85→    let vx1, vy1, vz1 = vertex_positions.((edge + 1) mod nv) in
    86→    let v1 = [| vx1; vy1; vz1 |] in
    87→    let edge_i = wrap32 (v1.(i) - v0.(i)) in
    88→    let edge_j = wrap32 (v1.(j) - v0.(j)) in
    89→    let chk_i = wrap32 (check_i - v0.(i)) in
    90→    let chk_j = wrap32 (check_j - v0.(j)) in
    91→    let d = wrap32 (fixmul ~a:chk_i ~b:edge_j - fixmul ~a:chk_j ~b:edge_i) in
    92→    if d < 0 then edgemask := !edgemask lor (1 lsl edge)
    93→  done;
    94→  !edgemask
    95→;;
    96→
    97→(* Check if a sphere (center pnt, radius rad) intersects a face.
    98→   Returns IT_FACE, IT_EDGE, IT_POINT, or IT_NONE. *)
    99→let check_sphere_to_face ~pnt ~norm ~nv ~rad ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
   100→  let open Ox_math in
   101→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in
   102→  if edgemask = 0
   103→  then it_face
   104→  else (
   105→    (* Find first set bit *)
   106→    let em = ref edgemask in
   107→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   108→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   109→      em := !em asr 1;
   110→      incr edgenum
   111→    done;
   112→    let v0 = vertex_positions.(!edgenum) in
   113→    let v1 = vertex_positions.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   114→    let checkvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:pnt ~b:v0 in
   115→    let edgelen, edgevec = vm_vec_normalized_dir ~v_end:v1 ~v_start:v0 in
   116→    let d = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:edgevec ~b:checkvec in
   117→    if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
   118→    then it_none
   119→    else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
   120→    then it_none
   121→    else (
   122→      let itype = ref it_point in
   123→      let closest_point =
   124→        if d < 0
   125→        then v0
   126→        else if d > edgelen
   127→        then v1
   128→        else (
   129→          itype := it_edge;
   130→          vm_vec_scale_add ~a:v0 ~b:edgevec ~k:d)
   131→      in
   132→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:pnt ~b:closest_point in
   133→      if dist <= rad then if !itype = it_point then it_none else !itype else it_none))
   134→;;
   135→
   136→(* 3x3 matrix determinant — all intermediate sums wrap to 32-bit like C *)
   137→let calc_det_value ~rvec:(rx, ry, rz) ~uvec:(ux, uy, uz) ~fvec:(fx, fy, fz) =
   138→  let open Ox_math in
   139→  let a = fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fz) in
   140→  let b = wrap32 (a - fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fy)) in
   141→  let c = wrap32 (b - fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fz)) in
   142→  let d = wrap32 (c + fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fx)) in
   143→  let e = wrap32 (d + fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fy)) in
   144→  wrap32 (e - fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fx))
   145→;;
   146→
   147→(* Line-line closest approach. Returns (ok, t1, t2).
   148→   Lines: p1 + t*v1 and p2 + t*v2. *)
   149→let check_line_to_line ~p1 ~v1 ~p2 ~v2 =
   150→  let open Ox_math in
   151→  let rvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p2 ~b:p1 in
   152→  let fvec = vm_vec_crossprod ~a:v1 ~b:v2 in
   153→  let cross_mag2 = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:fvec ~b:fvec in
   154→  if cross_mag2 = 0
   155→  then false, 0, 0 (* parallel *)
   156→  else (
   157→    let d1 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v2 ~fvec in
   158→    if oflow_check ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2
   159→    then false, 0, 0
   160→    else (
   161→      let t1 = fixdiv ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   162→      let d2 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v1 ~fvec in
   163→      if oflow_check ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2
   164→      then false, 0, 0
   165→      else (
   166→        let t2 = fixdiv ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   167→        true, t1, t2)))
   168→;;
   169→
   170→(* Check if line p0->p1 intersects a face. Returns (hit_type, newp).
   171→   vertex_list is the 6-element vertex index list from create_abs_vertex_lists.
   172→   facenum selects which face (0 or 1 for triangulated sides).
   173→   norm is the face normal for this facenum.
   174→   seg_verts/seg_vert_positions supply vertex position lookup. *)
   175→let check_line_to_face
   176→      ~p0
   177→      ~p1
   178→      ~norm
   179→      ~rad
   180→      ~facenum
   181→      ~nv
   182→      ~num_faces
   183→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   184→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   185→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   186→  =
   187→  let open Ox_math in
   188→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   189→  (* Use lowest point number as plane point *)
   190→  let vertnum =
   191→    if num_faces = 2
   192→    then min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2)
   193→    else (
   194→      let v = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   195→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   196→        if vertex_list.(i) < !v then v := vertex_list.(i)
   197→      done;
   198→      !v)
   199→  in
   200→  let plane_pnt = vpos vertnum in
   201→  match find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm:norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad with
   202→  | None -> it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   203→  | Some (_k, newp) ->
   204→    let checkp =
   205→      if rad <> 0 then vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:newp ~src:norm ~k:(-rad) else newp
   206→    in
   207→    (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   208→    let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   209→    let hit =
   210→      check_sphere_to_face ~pnt:checkp ~norm ~nv ~rad ~vertex_positions:face_verts
   211→    in
   212→    hit, newp
   213→;;
   214→
   215→(* special_check_line_to_face: handles the case where both start and end
   216→   positions poke through the plane of a side. *)
   217→let special_check_line_to_face
   218→      ~p0
   219→      ~p1
   220→      ~norm
   221→      ~rad
   222→      ~facenum
   223→      ~nv
   224→      ~num_faces
   225→      ~side_type:_
   226→      ~sidenum:_
   227→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   228→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   229→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   230→  =
   231→  let open Ox_math in
   232→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   233→  (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   234→  let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   235→  (* Figure out which edge(s) to check against *)
   236→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:p0 ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions:face_verts in
   237→  if edgemask = 0
   238→  then
   239→    (* Start point is inside face — fall back to check_line_to_face *)
   240→    check_line_to_face
   241→      ~p0
   242→      ~p1
   243→      ~norm
   244→      ~rad
   245→      ~facenum
   246→      ~nv
   247→      ~num_faces
   248→      ~vertex_list
   249→      ~seg_verts
   250→      ~seg_vert_positions
   251→  else (
   252→    let em = ref edgemask in
   253→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   254→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   255→      em := !em asr 1;
   256→      incr edgenum
   257→    done;
   258→    let edge_v0 = face_verts.(!edgenum) in
   259→    let edge_v1 = face_verts.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   260→    let edge_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:edge_v1 ~b:edge_v0 in
   261→    let move_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   262→    let edge_len, edge_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:edge_vec in
   263→    let move_len, move_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:move_vec in
   264→    let ok, edge_t, move_t =
   265→      check_line_to_line ~p1:edge_v0 ~v1:edge_vec_n ~p2:p0 ~v2:move_vec_n
   266→    in
   267→    if not ok
   268→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   269→    else if move_t < 0 || move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)
   270→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   271→    else (
   272→      let move_t2 = if move_t > move_len then move_len else move_t in
   273→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t < 0 then 0 else edge_t in
   274→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t2 > edge_len then edge_len else edge_t2 in
   275→      let closest_point_edge = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:edge_v0 ~b:edge_vec_n ~k:edge_t2 in
   276→      let closest_point_move = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:move_t2 in
   277→      let closest_dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point_edge ~b:closest_point_move in
   278→      if closest_dist < wrap32 (rad * 15) / 20
   279→      then (
   280→        let newp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:(wrap32 (move_t - rad)) in
   281→        it_edge, newp)
   282→      else it_none, (0, 0, 0)))
   283→;;
   284→
   285→(* Ray-sphere intersection. Returns (dist, intp) where dist=0 means no hit. *)
   286→let check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos ~sphere_rad =
   287→  let open Ox_math in
   288→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   289→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:sphere_pos ~b:p0 in
   290→  let mag_d, dn = vm_vec_copy_normalize ~v:d in
   291→  if mag_d = 0
   292→  then (
   293→    let int_dist = vm_vec_mag ~v:w in
   294→    if int_dist < sphere_rad then int_dist, p0 else 0, p0)
   295→  else (
   296→    let w_dist = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:dn ~b:w in
   297→    if w_dist < 0
   298→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* moving away *)
   299→    else if w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)
   300→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)
   301→    else (
   302→      let closest_point = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:w_dist in
   303→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point ~b:sphere_pos in
   304→      if dist < sphere_rad
   305→      then (
   306→        let dist2 = fixmul ~a:dist ~b:dist in
   307→        let rad2 = fixmul ~a:sphere_rad ~b:sphere_rad in
   308→        let shorten = fix_sqrt ~a:(wrap32 (rad2 - dist2)) in
   309→        let int_dist = wrap32 (w_dist - shorten) in
   310→        if int_dist > mag_d || int_dist < 0
   311→        then 1, p0 (* inside sphere *)
   312→        else (
   313→          let intp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:int_dist in
   314→          int_dist, intp))
   315→      else 0, (0, 0, 0)))
   316→;;
   317→
   318→(* Object type constants matching object.h *)
   319→let obj_robot = 2
   320→let obj_player = 4
   321→let obj_weapon = 5
   322→
   323→(* Check vector-to-object intersection with adjusted collision radius.
   324→   C original: fvi.cpp check_vector_to_object (D1+D2, identical)
   325→
   326→   Adjusts the target object's size based on type:
   327→   - Melee robots (attack_type != 0): size *= 3/4
   328→   - Players bumping into other players or coop weapon: size /= 2
   329→
   330→   Inputs:
   331→     p0, p1          - line segment endpoints (vec3)
   332→     rad              - collision radius from the moving object
   333→     obj_pos          - target object position (vec3)
   334→     obj_size         - target object size (fix)
   335→     obj_type         - target object type (OBJ_ROBOT, OBJ_PLAYER, etc.)
   336→     attack_type      - Robot_info[obj->id].attack_type (0 if not robot)
   337→     otherobj_type    - other object's type
   338→     game_mode_coop   - true if (Game_mode & GM_MULTI_COOP)
   339→     otherobj_parent_type - otherobj->ctype.laser_info.parent_type
   340→
   341→   Returns: (distance, intersection_point) — 0 distance means no hit *)
   342→let check_vector_to_object
   343→      ~p0
   344→      ~p1
   345→      ~rad
   346→      ~obj_pos
   347→      ~obj_size
   348→      ~obj_type
   349→      ~attack_type
   350→      ~otherobj_type
   351→      ~game_mode_coop
   352→      ~otherobj_parent_type
   353→  =
   354→  let size = ref obj_size in
   355→  if obj_type = obj_robot && attack_type <> 0 then size := !size * 3 / 4;
   356→  if
   357→    obj_type = obj_player
   358→    && (otherobj_type = obj_player
   359→        || (game_mode_coop
   360→            && otherobj_type = obj_weapon
   361→            && otherobj_parent_type = obj_player))
   362→  then size := !size / 2;
   363→  check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos:obj_pos ~sphere_rad:(!size + rad)
   364→;;

   428→(* Unpack a segment block from the packed array *)
   429→let unpack_seg (arr : int array) ~seg_base =
   430→  let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + i)) in
   431→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 6 + i)) in
   432→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 12 + i)) in
   433→  let normals =
   434→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   435→      let off = seg_base + 20 + (i * 3) in
   436→      arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   437→  in
   438→  let seg_vert_positions =
   439→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   440→      let off = seg_base + 56 + (i * 3) in
   441→      arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   442→  in
   443→  let wid = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 80 + i)) in
   444→  let first_object = arr.(seg_base + 86) in
   445→  children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions, wid, first_object
   446→;;

97→(* Check if a sphere (center pnt, radius rad) intersects a face.
    98→   Returns IT_FACE, IT_EDGE, IT_POINT, or IT_NONE. *)
    99→let check_sphere_to_face ~pnt ~norm ~nv ~rad ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
   100→  let open Ox_math in
   101→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in
   102→  if edgemask = 0
   103→  then it_face
   104→  else (
   105→    (* Find first set bit *)
   106→    let em = ref edgemask in
   107→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   108→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   109→      em := !em asr 1;
   110→      incr edgenum
   111→    done;
   112→    let v0 = vertex_positions.(!edgenum) in
   113→    let v1 = vertex_positions.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   114→    let checkvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:pnt ~b:v0 in
   115→    let edgelen, edgevec = vm_vec_normalized_dir ~v_end:v1 ~v_start:v0 in
   116→    let d = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:edgevec ~b:checkvec in
   117→    if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
   118→    then it_none
   119→    else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
   120→    then it_none
   121→    else (
   122→      let itype = ref it_point in
   123→      let closest_point =
   124→        if d < 0
   125→        then v0
   126→        else if d > edgelen
   127→        then v1
   128→        else (
   129→          itype := it_edge;
   130→          vm_vec_scale_add ~a:v0 ~b:edgevec ~k:d)
   131→      in
   132→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:pnt ~b:closest_point in
   133→      if dist <= rad then if !itype = it_point then it_none else !itype else it_none))
   134→;;
   135→
   136→(* 3x3 matrix determinant — all intermediate sums wrap to 32-bit like C *)
   137→let calc_det_value ~rvec:(rx, ry, rz) ~uvec:(ux, uy, uz) ~fvec:(fx, fy, fz) =
   138→  let open Ox_math in
   139→  let a = fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fz) in
   140→  let b = wrap32 (a - fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fy)) in
   141→  let c = wrap32 (b - fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fz)) in
   142→  let d = wrap32 (c + fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fx)) in
   143→  let e = wrap32 (d + fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fy)) in
   144→  wrap32 (e - fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fx))
   145→;;
   146→
   147→(* Line-line closest approach. Returns (ok, t1, t2).
   148→   Lines: p1 + t*v1 and p2 + t*v2. *)
   149→let check_line_to_line ~p1 ~v1 ~p2 ~v2 =
   150→  let open Ox_math in
   151→  let rvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p2 ~b:p1 in
   152→  let fvec = vm_vec_crossprod ~a:v1 ~b:v2 in
   153→  let cross_mag2 = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:fvec ~b:fvec in
   154→  if cross_mag2 = 0
   155→  then false, 0, 0 (* parallel *)
   156→  else (
   157→    let d1 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v2 ~fvec in
   158→    if oflow_check ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2
   159→    then false, 0, 0
   160→    else (
   161→      let t1 = fixdiv ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   162→      let d2 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v1 ~fvec in
   163→      if oflow_check ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2
   164→      then false, 0, 0
   165→      else (
   166→        let t2 = fixdiv ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   167→        true, t1, t2)))
   168→;;
   169→
   170→(* Check if line p0->p1 intersects a face. Returns (hit_type, newp).
   171→   vertex_list is the 6-element vertex index list from create_abs_vertex_lists.
   172→   facenum selects which face (0 or 1 for triangulated sides).
   173→   norm is the face normal for this facenum.
   174→   seg_verts/seg_vert_positions supply vertex position lookup. *)
   175→let check_line_to_face
   176→      ~p0
   177→      ~p1
   178→      ~norm
   179→      ~rad
   180→      ~facenum
   181→      ~nv
   182→      ~num_faces
   183→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   184→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   185→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   186→  =
   187→  let open Ox_math in
   188→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   189→  (* Use lowest point number as plane point *)
   190→  let vertnum =
   191→    if num_faces = 2
   192→    then min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2)
   193→    else (
   194→      let v = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   195→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   196→        if vertex_list.(i) < !v then v := vertex_list.(i)
   197→      done;
   198→      !v)
   199→  in
   200→  let plane_pnt = vpos vertnum in
   201→  match find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm:norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad with
   202→  | None -> it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   203→  | Some (_k, newp) ->
   204→    let checkp =
   205→      if rad <> 0 then vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:newp ~src:norm ~k:(-rad) else newp
   206→    in
   207→    (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   208→    let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   209→    let hit =
   210→      check_sphere_to_face ~pnt:checkp ~norm ~nv ~rad ~vertex_positions:face_verts
   211→    in
   212→    hit, newp
   213→;;
   214→
   215→(* special_check_line_to_face: handles the case where both start and end
   216→   positions poke through the plane of a side. *)
   217→let special_check_line_to_face
   218→      ~p0
   219→      ~p1
   220→      ~norm
   221→      ~rad
   222→      ~facenum
   223→      ~nv
   224→      ~num_faces
   225→      ~side_type:_
   226→      ~sidenum:_
   227→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   228→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   229→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   230→  =
   231→  let open Ox_math in
   232→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   233→  (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   234→  let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   235→  (* Figure out which edge(s) to check against *)
   236→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:p0 ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions:face_verts in
   237→  if edgemask = 0
   238→  then
   239→    (* Start point is inside face — fall back to check_line_to_face *)
   240→    check_line_to_face
   241→      ~p0
   242→      ~p1
   243→      ~norm
   244→      ~rad
   245→      ~facenum
   246→      ~nv
   247→      ~num_faces
   248→      ~vertex_list
   249→      ~seg_verts
   250→      ~seg_vert_positions
   251→  else (
   252→    let em = ref edgemask in
   253→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   254→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   255→      em := !em asr 1;
   256→      incr edgenum
   257→    done;
   258→    let edge_v0 = face_verts.(!edgenum) in
   259→    let edge_v1 = face_verts.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   260→    let edge_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:edge_v1 ~b:edge_v0 in
   261→    let move_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   262→    let edge_len, edge_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:edge_vec in
   263→    let move_len, move_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:move_vec in
   264→    let ok, edge_t, move_t =
   265→      check_line_to_line ~p1:edge_v0 ~v1:edge_vec_n ~p2:p0 ~v2:move_vec_n
   266→    in
   267→    if not ok
   268→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   269→    else if move_t < 0 || move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)
   270→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   271→    else (
   272→      let move_t2 = if move_t > move_len then move_len else move_t in
   273→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t < 0 then 0 else edge_t in
   274→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t2 > edge_len then edge_len else edge_t2 in
   275→      let closest_point_edge = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:edge_v0 ~b:edge_vec_n ~k:edge_t2 in
   276→      let closest_point_move = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:move_t2 in
   277→      let closest_dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point_edge ~b:closest_point_move in
   278→      if closest_dist < wrap32 (rad * 15) / 20
   279→      then (
   280→        let newp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:(wrap32 (move_t - rad)) in
   281→        it_edge, newp)
   282→      else it_none, (0, 0, 0)))
   283→;;

   285→(* Ray-sphere intersection. Returns (dist, intp) where dist=0 means no hit. *)
   286→let check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos ~sphere_rad =
   287→  let open Ox_math in
   288→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   289→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:sphere_pos ~b:p0 in
   290→  let mag_d, dn = vm_vec_copy_normalize ~v:d in
   291→  if mag_d = 0
   292→  then (
   293→    let int_dist = vm_vec_mag ~v:w in
   294→    if int_dist < sphere_rad then int_dist, p0 else 0, p0)
   295→  else (
   296→    let w_dist = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:dn ~b:w in
   297→    if w_dist < 0
   298→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* moving away *)
   299→    else if w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)
   300→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)
   301→    else (
   302→      let closest_point = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:w_dist in
   303→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point ~b:sphere_pos in
   304→      if dist < sphere_rad
   305→      then (
   306→        let dist2 = fixmul ~a:dist ~b:dist in
   307→        let rad2 = fixmul ~a:sphere_rad ~b:sphere_rad in
   308→        let shorten = fix_sqrt ~a:(wrap32 (rad2 - dist2)) in
   309→        let int_dist = wrap32 (w_dist - shorten) in
   310→        if int_dist > mag_d || int_dist < 0
   311→        then 1, p0 (* inside sphere *)
   312→        else (
   313→          let intp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:int_dist in
   314→          int_dist, intp))
   315→      else 0, (0, 0, 0)))
   316→;;

   946→let find_vector_intersection_v2 (arr : int array) =
   947→  let p0 = arr.(h_p0x), arr.(h_p0y), arr.(h_p0z) in
   948→  let startseg = arr.(h_startseg) in
   949→  let p1 = arr.(h_p1x), arr.(h_p1y), arr.(h_p1z) in
   950→  let rad = arr.(h_rad) in
   951→  let thisobjnum = arr.(h_thisobjnum) in
   952→  let flags = arr.(h_flags) in
   953→  let n_segments = arr.(h_n_segments) in
   954→  let n_objects = arr.(h_n_objects) in
   955→  let player_objnum = arr.(h_player_objnum) in
   956→  let physics_cheat = arr.(h_physics_cheat) <> 0 in
   957→  let game_mode_coop = arr.(h_game_mode_coop) <> 0 in
   958→  let game_time = arr.(h_game_time) in
   959→  let is_d2 = arr.(h_is_d2) <> 0 in
   960→  let ignore_count = arr.(h_ignore_obj_count) in
   961→  let ignore_obj_list = Array.init ignore_count ~f:(fun i -> arr.(h_ignore_obj_list + i)) in
   962→  let collision_table = Effect.perform Fetch_collision_table in
   963→  (* Validate startseg *)
   964→  let start_seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data startseg) in
   965→  let seg_verts, side_types, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   966→    unpack_seg_for_masks start_seg_data
   967→  in
   968→  let _, _, centermask =
   969→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   970→      ~checkp:p0 ~rad:0 ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   971→  in
   972→  if centermask <> 0
   973→  then (
   974→    let p0x, p0y, p0z = p0 in
   975→    [| hit_bad_p0; p0x; p0y; p0z; startseg; 0; -1; 0; 0; 0; 0; 0 |])
   976→  else (
   977→    let st = make_fvi_state () in
   978→    st.segs_visited.(0) <- startseg;
   979→    st.n_segs_visited <- 1;
   980→    st.fvi_hit_seg2 <- -1;
   981→    let hit_type, hit_pnt, hit_seg2, seglist, n_segs =
   982→      fvi_sub_v2
   983→        ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time ~ignore_obj_list
   984→        ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
   985→        st ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)
   986→    in
   987→    let hit_pnt_x = ref (let x, _, _ = hit_pnt in x) in
   988→    let hit_pnt_y = ref (let _, y, _ = hit_pnt in y) in
   989→    let hit_pnt_z = ref (let _, _, z = hit_pnt in z) in
   990→    let cur_hit_pnt () = (!hit_pnt_x, !hit_pnt_y, !hit_pnt_z) in
   991→    (* Resolve hit_seg *)
   992→    let hit_seg = ref (-1) in
   993→    if hit_seg2 <> -1
   994→    then (
   995→      let sd2 = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data hit_seg2) in
   996→      let sv2, st2, n2, svp2 = unpack_seg_for_masks sd2 in
   997→      let _, _, cm2 =
   998→        Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   999→          ~checkp:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~rad:0 ~seg_verts:sv2 ~side_types:st2 ~normals:n2 ~seg_vert_positions:svp2
  1000→      in
  1001→      if cm2 = 0 then hit_seg := hit_seg2);
  1002→    if !hit_seg = -1
  1003→    then
  1004→      hit_seg := find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~segnum:startseg ~n_segments;
  1005→    (* HACK: if HIT_WALL and hit_seg=-1, try fvi_hit_seg2 *)

  1023→        fvi_sub_v2

  1025→          ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
  1026→          st2 ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad:0 ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)

  1057→    let wnx, wny, wnz = st.wall_norm in

  1067→    result.(7) <- st.fvi_hit_object;

  1078→(* sphere_intersects_wall_v2: uses on-demand effects.
  1079→   Takes header: [pnt.xyz, rad, segnum, n_segments] (6 ints). *)
  1080→let sphere_intersects_wall_v2 (arr : int array) =
  1081→  let pnt = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
  1082→  let rad = arr.(3) in
  1083→  let start_segnum = arr.(4) in
  1084→  let n_segments = arr.(5) in
  1085→  let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
  1086→  let rec walk segnum =
  1087→    if segnum < 0 || segnum >= n_segments then 0
  1088→    else if visited.(segnum) then 0
  1089→    else (

Excellent! Now let me verify these exact differences. Let me create a detailed report with the exact line differences:

Based on my thorough analysis of the C and OCaml FVI implementations, I've found the following bugs that cause divergences between the FVI results:

## **CRITICAL BUGS FOUND - FVI C vs OCaml Comparison**

### **Bug 1: Unnecessary wrap32 wrapping on edge distance checks** 
**Location**: `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_fvi.ml` lines 117-120

**OCaml code**:
```ocaml
if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
then it_none
else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
```

**C code** (main_d2/fvi.cpp lines 269-271):
```c
if (d+rad < 0) return IT_NONE;
if (d-rad > edgelen) return IT_NONE;
```

**Problem**: The OCaml code wraps `d + rad` and `d - rad` in `check_sphere_to_face`, but C does NOT wrap these values. This causes different comparison semantics. When `d` and `rad` are large positive values, the wrapped sum might overflow to a negative number, changing the comparison result.

**Impact**: Causes edge detection failures in sphere-to-face collision checks, resulting in missing IT_EDGE hits or incorrectly returning IT_NONE.

---

### **Bug 2: Unnecessary wrap32 wrapping on move_len + rad**
**Location**: `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_fvi.ml` line 269

**OCaml code**:
```ocaml
else if move_t < 0 || move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)
```

**C code** (main_d2/fvi.cpp line 456):
```c
if (move_t<0 || move_t>move_len+rad)
```

Both `d` and `rad` are fixed-point values with F1_0 = 65536, so even large game distances would only reach a few million in fixed-point representation, well below INT32_MAX. let open Ox_math in
  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in
  if edgemask = 0
  then it_face
  else (
    (* Find first set bit *)
    let em = ref edgemask in
    let edgenum = ref 0 in
    while !em land 1 = 0 do
      em := !em asr 1;
      incr edgenum
    done;
    let v0 = vertex_positions.(!edgenum) in
    let v1 = vertex_positions.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
    let checkvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:pnt ~b:v0 in
    let edgelen, edgevec = vm_vec_normalized_dir ~v_end:v1 ~v_start:v0 in
    let d = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:edgevec ~b:checkvec in
    if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
    then it_none
    else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
    then it_none
    else (
      let itype = ref it_point in
      let closest_point =
        if d < 0
        then v0
        else if d > edgelen
        then v1
        else (
          itype := it_edge;
          vm_vec_scale_add ~a:v0 ~b:edgevec ~k:d)
      in
      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:pnt ~b:closest_point in
      if dist <= rad then if !itype = it_point then it_none else !itype else it_none))
;;

(* 3x3 matrix determinant — all intermediate sums wrap to 32-bit like C *)
let calc_det_value ~rvec:(rx, ry, rz) ~uvec:(ux, uy, uz) ~fvec:(fx, fy, fz) =
  let open Ox_math in
  let a = fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fz) in

(* Ray-sphere intersection. Returns (dist, intp) where dist=0 means no hit. *)
let check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos ~sphere_rad =
  let open Ox_math in
  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:sphere_pos ~b:p0 in
  let mag_d, dn = vm_vec_copy_normalize ~v:d in
  if mag_d = 0
  then (
    let int_dist = vm_vec_mag ~v:w in
    if int_dist < sphere_rad then int_dist, p0 else 0, p0)
  else (
    let w_dist = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:dn ~b:w in
    if w_dist < 0
    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* moving away *)
    else if w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)
    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)
    else (
      let closest_point = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:w_dist in
      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point ~b:sphere_pos in
      if dist < sphere_rad
      then (
        let dist2 = fixmul ~a:dist ~b:dist in
        let rad2 = fixmul ~a:sphere_rad ~b:sphere_rad in
        let shorten = fix_sqrt ~a:(wrap32 (rad2 - dist2)) in
        let int_dist = wrap32 (w_dist - shorten) in
        if int_dist > mag_d || int_dist < 0
        then 1, p0 (* inside sphere *)
        else (
          let intp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:int_dist in
          int_dist, intp))

   - ox_fvi.ml line 299: `wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)` vs C's `mag_d+sphere_rad`

6. I was in the process of verifying the wrap32 findings when the summary was requested. The key insight is that in C, these are 32-bit int operations that wrap on overflow. In OCaml (64-bit), without wrap32, the values wouldn't overflow the same way. However, the values (game distances + radii) shouldn't actually overflow 32 bits in practice, so wrap32 is likely unnecessary but also harmless.    - **ox/ox_ai_frame.ml** (2400+ lines)

     - Effect definitions at lines 140-177 (Ai_follow_path, Compute_vis_and_vec, Move_towards_segment_center, etc.)
     - AIM_CHASE_OBJECT (line 2199): Comment says "ai_move_relative_to_player on C side" but function is NOT called

   - **ox/ox_fvi.ml** (FVI implementation)
     - `check_sphere_to_face` (line 100+): Uses `wrap32(d + rad)` and `wrap32(d - rad)` where C uses plain `d+rad` and `d-rad`
     - `special_check_line_to_face` (line 269): Uses `wrap32(move_len + rad)` where C uses `move_len+rad`

   - **ox/ai_frame_bridge.ml** (lines 220-282)

   - **main_d2/ai.cpp** (bridge callsite)

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Investigated earliest divergence** (frame 43, object 92, pos/orient): Determined this is likely caused by FVI differences between C and OCaml implementations, since physics sim is OCaml-authoritative in ox build but C-ref uses C FVI
   - **Explored AI local divergences** (384725, largest category): Found these cascade from position/visibility differences
   - **Identified missing ai_move_relative_to_player call**: OCaml do_ai_frame doesn't call this for AIM_CHASE_OBJECT mode, but can't fix without bridge changes (prohibited)
   - **Found boss handling bug**: Missing compute_vis_and_vec before Do_boss_stuff, but low impact (bosses only)
   - **Currently investigating FVI wrap32 issue**: Found 4 places where OCaml uses wrap32 on additions that C does without wrapping. Need to determine if these cause actual divergences.

6. All user messages:
   - Initial task message with parity test results and detailed instructions for the ChocolateDescent porting workflow. Includes divergence data showing 650,468 divergences over 6,796 frames, absolute rules about not adding state syncing or modifying bridge files, and instructions to find either a bug to fix or a function to port.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Determine whether the FVI wrap32 differences are bugs causing divergences
   - If confirmed as bugs, fix the wrap32 issues in ox/ox_fvi.ml
   - If not bugs, identify another function to port or bug to fix
   - Build after any fix: `cd ox && sh ../scripts/ox/build_bridge.sh && cd .. && cmake --build build-ox -j8 --target headless_replay`
   - Commit with descriptive message

8. Current Work:
   I was investigating potential bugs in the OCaml FVI implementation (ox/ox_fvi.ml) that could cause the earliest parity divergence at frame 43, object 92. Specifically, I found 4 places where OCaml uses `wrap32` on additions that C performs without wrapping:
   
   1. Line 117: `if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0` vs C's `if (d+rad < 0)`
   2. Line 119: `else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen` vs C's `if (d-rad > edgelen)`
   3. Line 269: `move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)` vs C's `move_t>move_len+rad`
   4. Line 299: `w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)` vs C's `w_dist > mag_d+sphere_rad`
   
   I had just verified the C code at fvi.cpp line 269 confirming `d+rad < 0` without wrapping, and was about to determine whether these wrap32 calls are correct (needed for 32-bit overflow matching) or incorrect (unnecessary wrapping that changes comparison semantics). The key question: do these values actually overflow 32 bits in practice? If not, wrap32 is harmless but unnecessary. If the values are always within 32-bit range, then wrap32 and no-wrap32 produce identical results, and this is NOT the source of divergence.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Continue verifying whether the FVI wrap32 differences actually cause divergent behavior. Read the full C `check_sphere_to_face` and `special_check_line_to_face` functions to understand value ranges for `d`, `rad`, `move_len`, `mag_d`, `sphere_rad`. If these can't overflow 32 bits (which is likely for normal game coordinates), then the wrap32 calls are harmless and NOT the source of divergence, and I need to look elsewhere — potentially at deeper FVI algorithmic differences or at other functions to port. If they CAN overflow, removing the unnecessary wrap32 calls would be the fix.

1→(* FVI (Find Vector Intersection) pure geometry functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d1/fvi.cpp. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effects for on-demand data fetching. OCaml FVI fetches segment/object
     5→   data only for segments actually visited, instead of pre-packing everything. *)
     6→type _ Effect.t +=
     7→  | Fetch_object_data : int -> int array Effect.t
     8→  | Fetch_collision_table : int array Effect.t
     9→
    10→type vec3 = int * int * int
    11→
    12→let ij_table = [| [| 2; 1 |]; [| 0; 2 |]; [| 1; 0 |] |]
    13→let it_none = 0
    14→let it_face = 1
    15→let it_edge = 2
    16→let it_point = 3
    17→
    18→(* Wrap native int to signed 32-bit range, matching C int32_t overflow. *)
    19→let wrap32 x = Ox_math.wrap_i64_to_fix (Int64.of_int x)
    20→
    21→(* Overflow guard: returns true if abs(a)*abs(b) would overflow bit 47+. *)
    22→let oflow_check ~a ~b =
    23→  let tmp = Int64.(abs (of_int a) * abs (of_int b)) in
    24→  Int64.(shift_right tmp 47 <> 0L)
    25→;;
    26→
    27→(* Find where line p0->p1 intersects plane (plane_pnt, plane_norm),
    28→   accounting for radius offset. Returns Some newp if found, None if parallel/no-hit. *)
    29→let find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad =
    30→  let open Ox_math in
    31→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
    32→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:p0 ~b:plane_pnt in
    33→  let num_raw = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:w in
    34→  let den = wrap32 (-vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:d) in
    35→  let num = wrap32 (num_raw - rad) in
    36→  (* Check for various bad values — C operator precedence:
    37→     (den == 0) || ((den > 0) && ((num > den) || ((-num >> 15) >= den)))
    38→                 || (den < 0 && num < den) *)
    39→  let neg_num = wrap32 (-num) in
    40→  if den = 0
    41→  then None
    42→  else if den > 0 && (num > den || neg_num asr 15 >= den)
    43→  then None
    44→  else if den < 0 && num < den
    45→  then None
    46→  else (
    47→    let f1_0 = 0x10000 in
    48→    if abs num / (f1_0 / 2) >= abs den
    49→    then None (* would overflow *)
    50→    else (
    51→      let k = fixdiv ~a:num ~b:den in
    52→      let dx, dy, dz = d in
    53→      if oflow_check ~a:dx ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dy ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dz ~b:k
    54→      then None
    55→      else (
    56→        let scaled = vm_vec_scale2 ~v:d ~n:num ~d:den in
    57→        let newp = vm_vec_add ~a:p0 ~b:scaled in
    58→        Some (k, newp))))
    59→;;
    60→
    61→(* 2D point-in-polygon test. Projects face onto a 2D plane using ij_table
    62→   based on the largest component of the normal. Returns an edgemask where
    63→   bit N is set if the point is outside edge N. *)
    64→let check_point_to_face ~checkp ~norm ~nv ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
    65→  let open Ox_math in
    66→  let cpx, cpy, cpz = checkp in
    67→  let cp = [| cpx; cpy; cpz |] in
    68→  let nx, ny, nz = norm in
    69→  let n = [| nx; ny; nz |] in
    70→  let tx = abs nx in
    71→  let ty = abs ny in
    72→  let tz = abs nz in
    73→  let biggest = if tx > ty then if tx > tz then 0 else 2 else if ty > tz then 1 else 2 in
    74→  let i, j =
    75→    if n.(biggest) > 0
    76→    then ij_table.(biggest).(0), ij_table.(biggest).(1)
    77→    else ij_table.(biggest).(1), ij_table.(biggest).(0)
    78→  in
    79→  let check_i = cp.(i) in
    80→  let check_j = cp.(j) in
    81→  let edgemask = ref 0 in
    82→  for edge = 0 to nv - 1 do
    83→    let vx0, vy0, vz0 = vertex_positions.(edge) in
    84→    let v0 = [| vx0; vy0; vz0 |] in
    85→    let vx1, vy1, vz1 = vertex_positions.((edge + 1) mod nv) in
    86→    let v1 = [| vx1; vy1; vz1 |] in
    87→    let edge_i = wrap32 (v1.(i) - v0.(i)) in
    88→    let edge_j = wrap32 (v1.(j) - v0.(j)) in
    89→    let chk_i = wrap32 (check_i - v0.(i)) in
    90→    let chk_j = wrap32 (check_j - v0.(j)) in
    91→    let d = wrap32 (fixmul ~a:chk_i ~b:edge_j - fixmul ~a:chk_j ~b:edge_i) in
    92→    if d < 0 then edgemask := !edgemask lor (1 lsl edge)
    93→  done;
    94→  !edgemask
    95→;;
    96→
    97→(* Check if a sphere (center pnt, radius rad) intersects a face.
    98→   Returns IT_FACE, IT_EDGE, IT_POINT, or IT_NONE. *)
    99→let check_sphere_to_face ~pnt ~norm ~nv ~rad ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
   100→  let open Ox_math in
   101→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in
   102→  if edgemask = 0
   103→  then it_face
   104→  else (
   105→    (* Find first set bit *)
   106→    let em = ref edgemask in
   107→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   108→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   109→      em := !em asr 1;
   110→      incr edgenum
   111→    done;
   112→    let v0 = vertex_positions.(!edgenum) in
   113→    let v1 = vertex_positions.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   114→    let checkvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:pnt ~b:v0 in
   115→    let edgelen, edgevec = vm_vec_normalized_dir ~v_end:v1 ~v_start:v0 in
   116→    let d = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:edgevec ~b:checkvec in
   117→    if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
   118→    then it_none
   119→    else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
   120→    then it_none
   121→    else (
   122→      let itype = ref it_point in
   123→      let closest_point =
   124→        if d < 0
   125→        then v0
   126→        else if d > edgelen
   127→        then v1
   128→        else (
   129→          itype := it_edge;
   130→          vm_vec_scale_add ~a:v0 ~b:edgevec ~k:d)
   131→      in
   132→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:pnt ~b:closest_point in
   133→      if dist <= rad then if !itype = it_point then it_none else !itype else it_none))
   134→;;
   135→
   136→(* 3x3 matrix determinant — all intermediate sums wrap to 32-bit like C *)
   137→let calc_det_value ~rvec:(rx, ry, rz) ~uvec:(ux, uy, uz) ~fvec:(fx, fy, fz) =
   138→  let open Ox_math in
   139→  let a = fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fz) in
   140→  let b = wrap32 (a - fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fy)) in
   141→  let c = wrap32 (b - fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fz)) in
   142→  let d = wrap32 (c + fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fx)) in
   143→  let e = wrap32 (d + fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fy)) in
   144→  wrap32 (e - fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fx))
   145→;;
   146→
   147→(* Line-line closest approach. Returns (ok, t1, t2).
   148→   Lines: p1 + t*v1 and p2 + t*v2. *)
   149→let check_line_to_line ~p1 ~v1 ~p2 ~v2 =
   150→  let open Ox_math in
   151→  let rvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p2 ~b:p1 in
   152→  let fvec = vm_vec_crossprod ~a:v1 ~b:v2 in
   153→  let cross_mag2 = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:fvec ~b:fvec in
   154→  if cross_mag2 = 0
   155→  then false, 0, 0 (* parallel *)
   156→  else (
   157→    let d1 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v2 ~fvec in
   158→    if oflow_check ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2
   159→    then false, 0, 0
   160→    else (
   161→      let t1 = fixdiv ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   162→      let d2 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v1 ~fvec in
   163→      if oflow_check ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2
   164→      then false, 0, 0
   165→      else (
   166→        let t2 = fixdiv ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   167→        true, t1, t2)))
   168→;;
   169→
   170→(* Check if line p0->p1 intersects a face. Returns (hit_type, newp).
   171→   vertex_list is the 6-element vertex index list from create_abs_vertex_lists.
   172→   facenum selects which face (0 or 1 for triangulated sides).
   173→   norm is the face normal for this facenum.
   174→   seg_verts/seg_vert_positions supply vertex position lookup. *)
   175→let check_line_to_face
   176→      ~p0
   177→      ~p1
   178→      ~norm
   179→      ~rad
   180→      ~facenum
   181→      ~nv
   182→      ~num_faces
   183→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   184→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   185→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   186→  =
   187→  let open Ox_math in
   188→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   189→  (* Use lowest point number as plane point *)
   190→  let vertnum =
   191→    if num_faces = 2
   192→    then min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2)
   193→    else (
   194→      let v = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   195→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   196→        if vertex_list.(i) < !v then v := vertex_list.(i)
   197→      done;
   198→      !v)
   199→  in
   200→  let plane_pnt = vpos vertnum in
   201→  match find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm:norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad with
   202→  | None -> it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   203→  | Some (_k, newp) ->
   204→    let checkp =
   205→      if rad <> 0 then vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:newp ~src:norm ~k:(-rad) else newp
   206→    in
   207→    (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   208→    let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   209→    let hit =
   210→      check_sphere_to_face ~pnt:checkp ~norm ~nv ~rad ~vertex_positions:face_verts
   211→    in
   212→    hit, newp
   213→;;
   214→
   215→(* special_check_line_to_face: handles the case where both start and end
   216→   positions poke through the plane of a side. *)
   217→let special_check_line_to_face
   218→      ~p0
   219→      ~p1
   220→      ~norm
   221→      ~rad
   222→      ~facenum
   223→      ~nv
   224→      ~num_faces
   225→      ~side_type:_
   226→      ~sidenum:_
   227→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   228→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   229→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   230→  =
   231→  let open Ox_math in
   232→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   233→  (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   234→  let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   235→  (* Figure out which edge(s) to check against *)
   236→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:p0 ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions:face_verts in
   237→  if edgemask = 0
   238→  then
   239→    (* Start point is inside face — fall back to check_line_to_face *)
   240→    check_line_to_face
   241→      ~p0
   242→      ~p1
   243→      ~norm
   244→      ~rad
   245→      ~facenum
   246→      ~nv
   247→      ~num_faces
   248→      ~vertex_list
   249→      ~seg_verts
   250→      ~seg_vert_positions
   251→  else (
   252→    let em = ref edgemask in
   253→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   254→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   255→      em := !em asr 1;
   256→      incr edgenum
   257→    done;
   258→    let edge_v0 = face_verts.(!edgenum) in
   259→    let edge_v1 = face_verts.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   260→    let edge_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:edge_v1 ~b:edge_v0 in
   261→    let move_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   262→    let edge_len, edge_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:edge_vec in
   263→    let move_len, move_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:move_vec in
   264→    let ok, edge_t, move_t =
   265→      check_line_to_line ~p1:edge_v0 ~v1:edge_vec_n ~p2:p0 ~v2:move_vec_n
   266→    in
   267→    if not ok
   268→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   269→    else if move_t < 0 || move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)
   270→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   271→    else (
   272→      let move_t2 = if move_t > move_len then move_len else move_t in
   273→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t < 0 then 0 else edge_t in
   274→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t2 > edge_len then edge_len else edge_t2 in
   275→      let closest_point_edge = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:edge_v0 ~b:edge_vec_n ~k:edge_t2 in
   276→      let closest_point_move = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:move_t2 in
   277→      let closest_dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point_edge ~b:closest_point_move in
   278→      if closest_dist < wrap32 (rad * 15) / 20
   279→      then (
   280→        let newp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:(wrap32 (move_t - rad)) in
   281→        it_edge, newp)
   282→      else it_none, (0, 0, 0)))
   283→;;
   284→
   285→(* Ray-sphere intersection. Returns (dist, intp) where dist=0 means no hit. *)
   286→let check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos ~sphere_rad =
   287→  let open Ox_math in
   288→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   289→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:sphere_pos ~b:p0 in
   290→  let mag_d, dn = vm_vec_copy_normalize ~v:d in
   291→  if mag_d = 0
   292→  then (
   293→    let int_dist = vm_vec_mag ~v:w in
   294→    if int_dist < sphere_rad then int_dist, p0 else 0, p0)
   295→  else (
   296→    let w_dist = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:dn ~b:w in
   297→    if w_dist < 0
   298→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* moving away *)
   299→    else if w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)
   300→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)
   301→    else (
   302→      let closest_point = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:w_dist in
   303→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point ~b:sphere_pos in
   304→      if dist < sphere_rad
   305→      then (
   306→        let dist2 = fixmul ~a:dist ~b:dist in
   307→        let rad2 = fixmul ~a:sphere_rad ~b:sphere_rad in
   308→        let shorten = fix_sqrt ~a:(wrap32 (rad2 - dist2)) in
   309→        let int_dist = wrap32 (w_dist - shorten) in
   310→        if int_dist > mag_d || int_dist < 0
   311→        then 1, p0 (* inside sphere *)
   312→        else (
   313→          let intp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:int_dist in
   314→          int_dist, intp))
   315→      else 0, (0, 0, 0)))
   316→;;
   317→
   318→(* Object type constants matching object.h *)
   319→let obj_robot = 2
   320→let obj_player = 4
   321→let obj_weapon = 5
   322→
   323→(* Check vector-to-object intersection with adjusted collision radius.
   324→   C original: fvi.cpp check_vector_to_object (D1+D2, identical)
   325→
   326→   Adjusts the target object's size based on type:
   327→   - Melee robots (attack_type != 0): size *= 3/4
   328→   - Players bumping into other players or coop weapon: size /= 2
   329→
   330→   Inputs:
   331→     p0, p1          - line segment endpoints (vec3)
   332→     rad              - collision radius from the moving object
   333→     obj_pos          - target object position (vec3)
   334→     obj_size         - target object size (fix)
   335→     obj_type         - target object type (OBJ_ROBOT, OBJ_PLAYER, etc.)
   336→     attack_type      - Robot_info[obj->id].attack_type (0 if not robot)
   337→     otherobj_type    - other object's type
   338→     game_mode_coop   - true if (Game_mode & GM_MULTI_COOP)
   339→     otherobj_parent_type - otherobj->ctype.laser_info.parent_type
   340→
   341→   Returns: (distance, intersection_point) — 0 distance means no hit *)
   342→let check_vector_to_object
   343→      ~p0
   344→      ~p1
   345→      ~rad
   346→      ~obj_pos
   347→      ~obj_size
   348→      ~obj_type
   349→      ~attack_type
   350→      ~otherobj_type
   351→      ~game_mode_coop
   352→      ~otherobj_parent_type
   353→  =
   354→  let size = ref obj_size in
   355→  if obj_type = obj_robot && attack_type <> 0 then size := !size * 3 / 4;
   356→  if
   357→    obj_type = obj_player
   358→    && (otherobj_type = obj_player
   359→        || (game_mode_coop
   360→            && otherobj_type = obj_weapon
   361→            && otherobj_parent_type = obj_player))
   362→  then size := !size / 2;
   363→  check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos:obj_pos ~sphere_rad:(!size + rad)
   364→;;

   508→(* check_laser_related using pre-fetched object data arrays *)
   509→let check_laser_related_v2 ~(obj_a : int array) ~objnum_a ~(obj_b : int array) ~objnum_b ~game_time ~is_d2 =
   510→  let packed =
   511→    [| objnum_a; obj_a.(0); obj_a.(1); obj_a.(12); obj_a.(9); obj_a.(10); obj_a.(11)
   512→     ; objnum_b; obj_b.(0); obj_b.(1); obj_b.(12); obj_b.(9); obj_b.(10); obj_b.(11)
   513→     ; game_time
   514→    |]
   515→  in
   516→  if is_d2 then Ox_collide.laser_are_related_d2 packed <> 0
   517→  else Ox_collide.laser_are_related_d1 packed <> 0
   518→;;
   519→
   520→(* Get collision result from a pre-fetched 256-element collision table *)
   521→let get_collision_result_v2 ~(collision_table : int array) ~type_a ~type_b =
   522→  if type_a >= max_object_types || type_b >= max_object_types
   523→     || type_a < 0 || type_b < 0
   524→  then result_nothing
   525→  else collision_table.(type_a * 16 + type_b)
   526→;;
   527→
   528→(* fvi_sub_v2: recursive FVI traversal using on-demand effects.
   529→   Fetches segment/object data via Fetch_segment_data / Fetch_object_data effects
   530→   instead of reading from a pre-packed array. *)
   531→let rec fvi_sub_v2
   532→          ~(collision_table : int array)
   533→          ~n_objects
   534→          ~is_d2
   535→          ~game_time
   536→          ~(ignore_obj_list : int array)
   537→          ~game_mode_coop
   538→          ~player_objnum
   539→          ~physics_cheat
   540→          (st : fvi_state)
   541→          ~p0
   542→          ~startseg
   543→          ~p1
   544→          ~rad
   545→          ~thisobjnum
   546→          ~flags
   547→          ~entry_seg
   548→  =
   549→  let open Ox_math in
   550→  (* C sets disable_new_fvi_stuff = (obj->type != OBJ_PLAYER) before calling FVI.
   551→     When true, special_check_line_to_face falls back to check_line_to_face.
   552→     We replicate this by checking thisobjnum vs player_objnum. *)
   553→  let disable_new_fvi_stuff = thisobjnum >= 0 && thisobjnum <> player_objnum in
   554→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data startseg) in
   555→  let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions, wid, first_object =
   556→    unpack_seg seg_data ~seg_base:0
   557→  in
   558→  let closest_d = ref 0x7fffffff in
   559→  let closest_hit_point = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   560→  let hit_type = ref hit_none in
   561→  let hit_seg = ref (-1) in
   562→  let hit_none_seg = ref (-1) in
   563→  let hit_none_n_segs = ref 0 in
   564→  let hit_none_seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
   565→  let cur_nest_level = st.fvi_nest_count in
   566→  let seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
   567→  let n_segs = ref 1 in
   568→  if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0 then seglist.(0) <- startseg;
   569→  st.fvi_nest_count <- st.fvi_nest_count + 1;
   570→  let quit = ref false in
   571→  (* Fetch thisobjnum data once for reuse *)
   572→  let thisobj_data =
   573→    if thisobjnum > -1 && thisobjnum < n_objects
   574→    then Some (Effect.perform (Fetch_object_data thisobjnum))
   575→    else None
   576→  in
   577→  let thisobj_type = match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(0) | None -> 0 in
   578→  let thisobj_attack = match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(13) | None -> 0 in
   579→  (* Check objects in this segment *)
   580→  if flags land fq_check_objs <> 0 && not !quit
   581→  then (
   582→    let objnum = ref first_object in
   583→    while !objnum <> -1 && not !quit do
   584→      let ob = !objnum in
   585→      if ob < 0 || ob >= n_objects then
   586→        objnum := -1
   587→      else
   588→      let ob_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_data ob) in
   589→      let ( obj_type_ob
   590→          , _obj_id_ob
   591→          , obj_flags_ob
   592→          , obj_pos_ob
   593→          , obj_size_ob
   594→          , next_in_seg
   595→          , parent_type_ob
   596→          , _parent_num_ob
   597→          , _parent_sig_ob
   598→          , _creation_time_ob
   599→          , _signature_ob
   600→          , attack_type_ob )
   601→        =
   602→        unpack_obj ob_data ~obj_base:0
   603→      in
   604→      let skip = ref false in
   605→      if obj_type_ob = obj_none then skip := true;
   606→      if obj_flags_ob land 2 <> 0 then skip := true;
   607→      if thisobjnum = ob then skip := true;
   608→      if (not !skip) && obj_in_ignore_list_v2 ~ignore_obj_list ~objnum:ob then skip := true;
   609→      if not !skip
   610→      then (
   611→        match thisobj_data with
   612→        | Some td ->
   613→          if check_laser_related_v2
   614→               ~obj_a:ob_data ~objnum_a:ob
   615→               ~obj_b:td ~objnum_b:thisobjnum
   616→               ~game_time ~is_d2
   617→          then skip := true
   618→        | None -> ());
   619→      if (not !skip) && thisobjnum > -1
   620→      then (
   621→        let cr1 =
   622→          get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:thisobj_type ~type_b:obj_type_ob
   623→        in
   624→        let cr2 =
   625→          get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:obj_type_ob ~type_b:thisobj_type
   626→        in
   627→        if cr1 = result_nothing && cr2 = result_nothing then skip := true);
   628→      if
   629→        (not !skip)
   630→        && is_d2
   631→        && obj_type_ob = obj_powerup
   632→        && flags land fq_ignore_powerups <> 0
   633→      then skip := true;
   634→      if (not !skip) && thisobjnum > -1
   635→      then (
   636→        if thisobj_type = obj_robot && obj_type_ob = obj_robot
   637→        then
   638→          if is_d2
   639→          then skip := true
   640→          else if not (attack_type_ob <> 0 && thisobj_attack <> 0)
   641→          then skip := true);
   642→      if not !skip
   643→      then (
   644→        let fudged_rad = ref rad in
   645→        if thisobjnum > -1
   646→        then (
   647→          if thisobj_type = obj_robot && thisobj_attack <> 0
   648→          then fudged_rad := rad * 3 / 4;
   649→          if
   650→            thisobj_type = obj_player
   651→            && (obj_type_ob = obj_player
   652→                || (game_mode_coop
   653→                    && obj_type_ob = obj_weapon
   654→                    && parent_type_ob = obj_player))
   655→          then fudged_rad := rad / 2);
   656→        let d, hit_point =
   657→          check_vector_to_object
   658→            ~p0
   659→            ~p1
   660→            ~rad:!fudged_rad
   661→            ~obj_pos:obj_pos_ob
   662→            ~obj_size:obj_size_ob
   663→            ~obj_type:obj_type_ob
   664→            ~attack_type:attack_type_ob
   665→            ~otherobj_type:thisobj_type
   666→            ~game_mode_coop
   667→            ~otherobj_parent_type:
   668→              (match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(8) | None -> 0)
   669→        in
   670→        if d <> 0 && d < !closest_d
   671→        then (
   672→          st.fvi_hit_object <- ob;
   673→          closest_d := d;
   674→          closest_hit_point := hit_point;
   675→          hit_type := hit_object));
   676→      objnum := next_in_seg
   677→    done);
   678→  (* HACK: if this object type doesn't collide with walls, set rad=0 *)
   679→  let rad =
   680→    if thisobjnum > -1
   681→    then (
   682→      let cr =
   683→        get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:thisobj_type ~type_b:0
   684→      in
   685→      if cr = result_nothing then 0 else rad)
   686→    else rad
   687→  in
   688→  (* Get start/end masks *)
   689→  let startmask =
   690→    let fm, _, _ =
   691→      Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   692→        ~checkp:p0 ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   693→    in
   694→    fm
   695→  in
   696→  let endmask, _sidemask, centermask =
   697→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   698→      ~checkp:p1 ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   699→  in
   700→  if centermask = 0 then hit_none_seg := startseg;
   701→  if endmask <> 0 && not !quit
   702→  then (
   703→    let bit = ref 1 in
   704→    let side = ref 0 in
   705→    while !side < 6 && endmask >= !bit && not !quit do
   706→      let sn = !side in
   707→      let num_faces_raw =
   708→        let st = side_types.(sn) in
   709→        if st = 1 then 1 else if st = 2 || st = 3 then 2 else 1
   710→      in
   711→      let num_faces = if num_faces_raw = 0 then 1 else num_faces_raw in
   712→      for face = 0 to 1 do
   713→        if (not !quit) && endmask land !bit <> 0
   714→        then
   715→          if children.(sn) <> entry_seg
   716→          then (
   717→            let nv = if num_faces = 1 then 4 else 3 in
   718→            let _nf, vertex_list =
   719→              Ox_gameseg.create_abs_vertex_lists
   720→                ~side_type:side_types.(sn) ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   721→            in
   722→            let norm = normals.((sn * 2) + face) in
   723→            let face_hit_type, hit_point =
   724→              if startmask land !bit <> 0 && not disable_new_fvi_stuff
   725→              then
   726→                special_check_line_to_face
   727→                  ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum:face ~nv ~num_faces
   728→                  ~side_type:side_types.(sn) ~sidenum:sn
   729→                  ~vertex_list ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions
   730→              else
   731→                check_line_to_face
   732→                  ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum:face ~nv ~num_faces
   733→                  ~vertex_list ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions
   734→            in
   735→            if face_hit_type <> 0

   746→              let is_trans =

   787→                        st

   796→                        closest_d := d;
   797→                        closest_hit_point := sub_hit_point;
   798→                        hit_type := sub_hit_type;
   799→                        if sub_hit_seg <> -1 then hit_seg := sub_hit_seg;
   800→                        if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   801→                        then (
   802→                          let ii = ref 0 in
   803→                          while !ii < temp_n_segs && !n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1 do
   804→                            seglist.(!n_segs) <- temp_seglist.(!ii);
   805→                            n_segs := !n_segs + 1;
   806→                            ii := !ii + 1
   807→                          done))
   808→                      else (
   809→                        st.wall_norm <- save_wall_norm;
   810→                        st.fvi_hit_object <- save_hit_objnum))
   811→                    else (
   812→                      st.wall_norm <- save_wall_norm;
   813→                      if sub_hit_seg <> -1 then hit_none_seg := sub_hit_seg;
   814→                      if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   815→                      then
   816→                        for ii = 0 to min (temp_n_segs - 1) (max_fvi_segs - 2) do
   817→                          hit_none_seglist.(ii) <- temp_seglist.(ii)
   818→                        done;
   819→                      hit_none_n_segs := temp_n_segs))))
   820→              else (
   821→                let d = vm_vec_dist ~a:hit_point ~b:p0 in
   822→                if d < !closest_d
   823→                then (
   824→                  closest_d := d;
   825→                  closest_hit_point := hit_point;
   826→                  hit_type := hit_wall;
   827→                  st.wall_norm <- normals.((sn * 2) + face);
   828→                  let _, _, cm =
   829→                    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   830→                      ~checkp:hit_point ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   831→                  in
   832→                  if cm = 0 then hit_seg := startseg else st.fvi_hit_seg2 <- startseg;
   833→                  st.fvi_hit_seg <- !hit_seg;
   834→                  st.fvi_hit_side <- sn;
   835→                  st.fvi_hit_side_seg <- startseg))));
   836→        bit := !bit lsl 1
   837→      done;
   838→      incr side
   839→    done);
   840→  if !hit_type = hit_none
   841→  then (
   842→    let intp = p1 in
   843→    let ints = !hit_none_seg in
   844→    if !hit_none_seg <> -1
   845→    then (
   846→      if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   847→      then
   848→        for i = 0 to !hit_none_n_segs - 1 do
   849→          if !n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1
   850→          then (
   851→            seglist.(!n_segs) <- hit_none_seglist.(i);
   852→            n_segs := !n_segs + 1)
   853→        done)
   854→    else if cur_nest_level <> 0
   855→    then n_segs := 0;
   856→    !hit_type, intp, ints, seglist, !n_segs)
   857→  else (
   858→    let intp = !closest_hit_point in
   859→    let ints =
   860→      if !hit_seg = -1
   861→      then if st.fvi_hit_seg2 <> -1 then st.fvi_hit_seg2 else !hit_none_seg
   862→      else !hit_seg
   863→    in
   864→    !hit_type, intp, ints, seglist, !n_segs)
   865→;;

   867→(* Unpack segment data for centermask check (returns just the fields needed) *)
   868→let unpack_seg_for_masks seg_data =
   869→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   870→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   871→  let normals =
   872→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   873→      let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   874→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   875→  in
   876→  let seg_vert_positions =
   877→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   878→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   879→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   880→  in
   881→  seg_verts, side_types, normals, seg_vert_positions
   882→;;
   883→
   884→(* find_point_seg_fvi_v2: uses Fetch_segment_data effects *)
   885→let find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments =
   886→  let fetch s =
   887→    let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data s) in
   888→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   889→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   890→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   891→    let normals =
   892→      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   893→        let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   894→        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   895→    in
   896→    let seg_vert_positions =
   897→      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   898→        let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   899→        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   900→    in
   901→    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   902→  in
   903→  let rec trace s iterations =
   904→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments then -1
   905→    else if iterations > 1024 then s
   906→    else (
   907→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
   908→      let centermask, side_dists =
   909→        Ox_gameseg.get_side_dists
   910→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz) ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   911→      in
   912→      if centermask = 0 then s
   913→      else (
   914→        (* Multi-branch DFS: try all children in order of most-negative side_dist,
   915→           matching C's trace_segs which retries with the next best child on failure *)
   916→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   917→        let result = ref (-1) in
   918→        let continue_ = ref true in
   919→        while !continue_ do
   920→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   921→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   922→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   923→            if centermask land (1 lsl sn) <> 0 && children.(sn) >= 0
   924→               && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   925→            then (
   926→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   927→              biggest_side := sn)
   928→          done;
   929→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   930→          then (
   931→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   932→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   933→            if check <> -1
   934→            then (
   935→              result := check;
   936→              continue_ := false))
   937→          else continue_ := false
   938→        done;
   939→        !result))
   940→  in
   941→  if segnum < 0 || segnum >= n_segments then -1 else trace segnum 0
   942→;;
   943→
   944→(* find_vector_intersection_v2: top-level FVI using on-demand effects.
   945→   Takes just the header array (18+N ints), fetches data via effects. *)
   946→let find_vector_intersection_v2 (arr : int array) =
   947→  let p0 = arr.(h_p0x), arr.(h_p0y), arr.(h_p0z) in
   948→  let startseg = arr.(h_startseg) in
   949→  let p1 = arr.(h_p1x), arr.(h_p1y), arr.(h_p1z) in
   950→  let rad = arr.(h_rad) in
   951→  let thisobjnum = arr.(h_thisobjnum) in
   952→  let flags = arr.(h_flags) in
   953→  let n_segments = arr.(h_n_segments) in
   954→  let n_objects = arr.(h_n_objects) in
   955→  let player_objnum = arr.(h_player_objnum) in
   956→  let physics_cheat = arr.(h_physics_cheat) <> 0 in
   957→  let game_mode_coop = arr.(h_game_mode_coop) <> 0 in
   958→  let game_time = arr.(h_game_time) in
   959→  let is_d2 = arr.(h_is_d2) <> 0 in
   960→  let ignore_count = arr.(h_ignore_obj_count) in
   961→  let ignore_obj_list = Array.init ignore_count ~f:(fun i -> arr.(h_ignore_obj_list + i)) in
   962→  let collision_table = Effect.perform Fetch_collision_table in
   963→  (* Validate startseg *)
   964→  let start_seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data startseg) in
   965→  let seg_verts, side_types, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   966→    unpack_seg_for_masks start_seg_data
   967→  in
   968→  let _, _, centermask =
   969→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   970→      ~checkp:p0 ~rad:0 ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   971→  in
   972→  if centermask <> 0
   973→  then (
   974→    let p0x, p0y, p0z = p0 in
   975→    [| hit_bad_p0; p0x; p0y; p0z; startseg; 0; -1; 0; 0; 0; 0; 0 |])
   976→  else (
   977→    let st = make_fvi_state () in
   978→    st.segs_visited.(0) <- startseg;
   979→    st.n_segs_visited <- 1;
   980→    st.fvi_hit_seg2 <- -1;
   981→    let hit_type, hit_pnt, hit_seg2, seglist, n_segs =
   982→      fvi_sub_v2
   983→        ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time ~ignore_obj_list
   984→        ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
   985→        st ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)
   986→    in
   987→    let hit_pnt_x = ref (let x, _, _ = hit_pnt in x) in
   988→    let hit_pnt_y = ref (let _, y, _ = hit_pnt in y) in
   989→    let hit_pnt_z = ref (let _, _, z = hit_pnt in z) in
   990→    let cur_hit_pnt () = (!hit_pnt_x, !hit_pnt_y, !hit_pnt_z) in
   991→    (* Resolve hit_seg *)
   992→    let hit_seg = ref (-1) in
   993→    if hit_seg2 <> -1
   994→    then (
   995→      let sd2 = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data hit_seg2) in
   996→      let sv2, st2, n2, svp2 = unpack_seg_for_masks sd2 in
   997→      let _, _, cm2 =
   998→        Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   999→          ~checkp:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~rad:0 ~seg_verts:sv2 ~side_types:st2 ~normals:n2 ~seg_vert_positions:svp2
  1000→      in
  1001→      if cm2 = 0 then hit_seg := hit_seg2);
  1002→    if !hit_seg = -1
  1003→    then
  1004→      hit_seg := find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~segnum:startseg ~n_segments;
  1005→    (* HACK: if HIT_WALL and hit_seg=-1, try fvi_hit_seg2 *)
  1006→    if hit_type = hit_wall && !hit_seg = -1 && st.fvi_hit_seg2 <> -1
  1007→    then (
  1008→      let sd2 = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data st.fvi_hit_seg2) in
  1009→      let sv2, st2, n2, svp2 = unpack_seg_for_masks sd2 in
  1010→      let _, _, cm2 =
  1011→        Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
  1012→          ~checkp:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~rad:0 ~seg_verts:sv2 ~side_types:st2 ~normals:n2 ~seg_vert_positions:svp2
  1013→      in
  1014→      if cm2 = 0 then hit_seg := st.fvi_hit_seg2);
  1015→    (* Retry with rad=0 if hit_seg still -1 *)
  1016→    if !hit_seg = -1
  1017→    then (
  1018→      let st2 = make_fvi_state () in
  1019→      st2.segs_visited.(0) <- startseg;
  1020→      st2.n_segs_visited <- 1;
  1021→      st2.fvi_hit_seg2 <- -1;
  1022→      let _new_hit_type, _new_hit_pnt, new_hit_seg2, _new_seglist, _new_n_segs =
  1023→        fvi_sub_v2
  1024→          ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time ~ignore_obj_list
  1025→          ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
  1026→          st2 ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad:0 ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)
  1027→      in
  1028→      if new_hit_seg2 <> -1
  1029→      then (
  1030→        hit_seg := new_hit_seg2;
  1031→        let npx, npy, npz = _new_hit_pnt in
  1032→        hit_pnt_x := npx;
  1033→        hit_pnt_y := npy;
  1034→        hit_pnt_z := npz));
  1035→    (* Trim seglist at hit point *)
  1036→    let final_seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
  1037→    let final_n_segs = ref n_segs in
  1038→    Array.blit ~src:seglist ~src_pos:0 ~dst:final_seglist ~dst_pos:0 ~len:n_segs;
  1039→    if !hit_seg <> -1 && flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
  1040→    then
  1041→      if
  1042→        !final_n_segs > 0
  1043→        && final_seglist.(!final_n_segs - 1) <> !hit_seg
  1044→        && !final_n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1
  1045→      then (
  1046→        final_seglist.(!final_n_segs) <- !hit_seg;
  1047→        final_n_segs := !final_n_segs + 1);
  1048→    if !hit_seg <> -1 && flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
  1049→    then (
  1050→      let found = ref false in
  1051→      for i = 0 to !final_n_segs - 1 do
  1052→        if (not !found) && final_seglist.(i) = !hit_seg
  1053→        then (
  1054→          final_n_segs := i + 1;
  1055→          found := true)
  1056→      done);
  1057→    let wnx, wny, wnz = st.wall_norm in
  1058→    let result_len = 12 + !final_n_segs in
  1059→    let result = Array.create ~len:result_len 0 in
  1060→    result.(0) <- hit_type;
  1061→    result.(1) <- !hit_pnt_x;
  1062→    result.(2) <- !hit_pnt_y;
  1063→    result.(3) <- !hit_pnt_z;
  1064→    result.(4) <- !hit_seg;
  1065→    result.(5) <- st.fvi_hit_side;
  1066→    result.(6) <- st.fvi_hit_side_seg;
  1067→    result.(7) <- st.fvi_hit_object;
  1068→    result.(8) <- wnx;
  1069→    result.(9) <- wny;
  1070→    result.(10) <- wnz;
  1071→    result.(11) <- !final_n_segs;
  1072→    for i = 0 to !final_n_segs - 1 do
  1073→      result.(12 + i) <- final_seglist.(i)
  1074→    done;
  1075→    result)
  1076→;;
